message:
fix(pane-tiers,rollout): reclassify tier before interval lookup; case-insensitive backend env parsing

Pane tiers:
- `effective_interval()` now calls `classify()` internally before looking
  up the interval, so callers get the current tier even if no explicit
  classify() was called since the last state change (e.g. set_background)
- `metrics()` now uses `compute_tier()` per pane instead of reading the
  cached `state.tier`, ensuring tier distribution counts reflect current
  activity state without requiring a prior classification sweep
- New tests verify both fixes: effective_interval reclassifies a
  background pane, and metrics compute tiers without prior classify()

TUI rollout:
- `parse_backend()` now normalizes the env var value by trimming
  whitespace and lowercasing before matching, so FT_TUI_BACKEND=" FTUI "
  or "Ratatui" work as expected instead of silently falling back to the
  stage default
- Tests updated from "case_sensitive"/"whitespace_not_trimmed" to
  "case_insensitive"/"trims_surrounding_whitespace" reflecting the new
  behavior

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
